cards.title: Hypertension — BNYS Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Assessment: BP both arms, 3 readings. Fundoscopy, urine albumin, ECG, serum creatinine. Rule out secondary HTN.
Naturopathic protocol (Stage 1: 130–139/80–89):
Diet: DASH diet — fruits, vegetables, low-fat dairy, reduced sodium (<2 g/day). Eliminate: processed food, excess alcohol, caffeine. Increase: potassium (banana, coconut water), magnesium (nuts, seeds), nitrate-rich foods (beetroot, green leafy).
Hydrotherapy: Cold hip bath 20 min daily. Neutral bath 97–98°F, 20 min (parasympathetic). Wet sheet pack weekly.
Yoga: Shavasana 30 min daily (most evidence). Nadi Shodhana pranayama 20 min. Avoid: inversions, Bhastrika, Kapalbhati.
Herbs: Sarpagandha (Rauwolfia serpentina) 250 mg OD (ONLY under physician supervision — avoid in depression, peptic ulcer). Arjuna (Terminalia arjuna) bark decoction. Garlic 600–1200 mg/day.
Massage: Head massage, foot reflexology (point: kidney zone) — reduces sympathetic tone.
Expected response: 8–12 weeks for significant BP reduction with lifestyle.

cards.title: Asthma — BNYS Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Assessment: Spirometry (FEV1/FVC <0.7), peak flow diary, allergy testing (skin prick or IgE), identify triggers.
Naturopathic protocol:
Breathing therapy: Buteyko technique — nasal breathing, reduced breathing volume, CO2 tolerance. Evidence: ↓ bronchodilator use by 86% (McHugh 2003). Pranayama: Anulom Vilom 20 min BD, Bhramari 10 min. Avoid: Bhastrika, Kapalbhati in acute asthma.
Hydrotherapy: Steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil (acute). Chest pack (cold compress on chest, hot on back) — reduces bronchospasm. Cold water sponging in summer.
Diet: Eliminate: milk, wheat, eggs, shellfish (common triggers). Increase: anti-inflammatory foods, ginger, turmeric, omega-3. Vitamin C 1000 mg/day (bronchial protection).
Herbs: Vasaka (Adhatoda vasica) leaf decoction — bronchodilator, mucolytic. Pushkarmool, Licorice (Yashtimadhu) — anti-inflammatory. Tylophora indica — significant evidence for asthma.
Environment: Remove carpets, mouldy items. HEPA filter. Avoid: tobacco smoke, strong perfumes, cold dry air.
Note: Never stop reliever inhalers. Naturopathy is adjunct to standard care.

cards.title: IBS — BNYS Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Subtypes: IBS-C (constipation), IBS-D (diarrhoea), IBS-M (mixed). Rome IV criteria: recurrent abdominal pain ≥1 day/week for 3 months + ≥2 of: pain related to defaecation, change in stool frequency, change in stool form.
Naturopathic protocol:
Diet: Low-FODMAP diet (gold standard — 70% response rate). Identify personal triggers. Increase soluble fibre (oats, psyllium husk 5–10 g/day with water). Avoid: gas-forming foods, carbonated drinks, sorbitol, caffeine excess.
Gut-directed hypnotherapy: Level 1A evidence. 7-session protocol (Whorwell). Equally effective as low-FODMAP.
Hydrotherapy: Abdominal hot pack (IBS-C). Cold abdominal compress (IBS-D). Sitz bath alternate hot-cold (IBS-M). Neem enema (infection-related IBS).
Yoga: Pawanmuktasana (wind-relieving pose), Apanasana, Supta Vajrasana. Yoga Nidra — reduces gut hypersensitivity via gut-brain axis.
Herbs: Peppermint oil enteric-coated 0.2–0.4 ml TDS (antispasmodic — Level 1A for IBS). Triphala (IBS-C). Bilva (Aegle marmelos) fruit powder (IBS-D). Probiotics: L. rhamnosus GG, B. infantis.
Stress management: Address gut-brain axis. MBSR, CBT.

cards.title: Anxiety & Depression — BNYS Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Assessment tools: PHQ-9 (depression), GAD-7 (anxiety), DASS-21. Baseline HRV. Rule out hypothyroidism, anaemia, B12 deficiency as causes.
Naturopathic protocol:
Exercise: 30 min aerobic 3–5x/week = antidepressant effect (Level 1A). Immediate anxiolytic effect from single session.
Yoga: Yoga Nidra 30 min daily (↓ cortisol, ↓ anxiety scores). Bhramari pranayama 10 min (immediate anxiolytic). Shavasana. Avoid: over-stimulating practices (Kapalbhati) in acute anxiety.
Hydrotherapy: Neutral bath 97°F, 20–30 min daily — sedating, reduces central sensitisation. Wet sheet pack weekly.
Dietary: Increase tryptophan-rich foods (banana, dates, milk, turkey). Omega-3 2–3 g/day (EPA-dominant for depression). Magnesium 300–400 mg. Eliminate: caffeine, alcohol, refined sugar.
Herbs: Ashwagandha (KSM-66) 300 mg BD — ↓ cortisol, ↓ anxiety (Level 2A). Brahmi (Bacopa monnieri) 300 mg BD — anxiety, memory, cognitive function. St John's Wort 300 mg TDS — mild-moderate depression (Level 1A, drug interactions — avoid with antidepressants, OCP). Passionflower for acute anxiety.
Refer: Suicidal ideation, severe symptoms, not responding to 8 weeks of naturopathic care.

cards.title: Arthritis — BNYS Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Types: OA (degenerative, non-inflammatory) vs RA (autoimmune, inflammatory). Naturopathic approach differs significantly.
Osteoarthritis protocol:
• Mud therapy: Local mud pack on affected joint (cold — anti-inflammatory in acute). Heat mud pack (chronic pain, stiffness).
• Hydrotherapy: Contrast bath for knees/hands — alternating hot (3 min) and cold (1 min) × 5 cycles.
• Yoga: Gentle ROM exercises, Vajrasana (knee OA), avoid weight-bearing asanas in acute flare.
• Supplements: Glucosamine 1500 mg/day, Chondroitin 1200 mg/day (Level 2A), Turmeric (BCM-95) 500 mg BD, Boswellia serrata 300 mg BD (strong evidence — ↓ pain, ↑ ROM).
Rheumatoid Arthritis protocol:
• Anti-inflammatory diet (eliminate nightshades, dairy — observe individual response).
• Omega-3 3–4 g/day (↓ tender joints, ↓ morning stiffness).
• Hydrotherapy: Warm wax bath for hands. Cold pack during flares.
• Herbs: Thunder god vine (Tripterygium wilfordii) — significant RA evidence, toxicity risk — physician only. Boswellia, Turmeric.
• Never stop DMARDs without rheumatologist guidance.

cards.title: PCOS — BNYS Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Diagnosis (Rotterdam criteria — 2 of 3): Oligo/anovulation, Clinical/biochemical hyperandrogenism, Polycystic ovaries on USS.
Naturopathic protocol:
Diet: Low-GI diet. Reduce refined carbohydrates. Increase: flaxseed (lignans reduce androgens), spearmint tea (2 cups/day — ↓ free testosterone), cinnamon (improves insulin sensitivity), cruciferous vegetables (oestrogen metabolism).
Inositol: Myo-inositol 4 g + D-chiro-inositol 100 mg daily (40:1 ratio). Best evidence for PCOS — ↓ insulin resistance, ↓ androgens, restores ovulation (Level 2A). Compare favourably to metformin.
Herbs: Shatavari (Asparagus racemosus) — hormonal balancing. Licorice + Peony — ↓ LH/FSH ratio. Tribulus terrestris — ovulation induction (limited evidence).
Yoga: Setu Bandhasana, Baddha Konasana, Supta Baddha Konasana — pelvic floor and reproductive health. Bhramari for HPA axis balance.
Hydrotherapy: Warm hip bath 3x/week — pelvic circulation. Cold hip bath (alternate days) — reduces oestrogen dominance.
Exercise: Resistance training primary (↑ insulin sensitivity, ↓ androgens). Avoid: chronic cardio (↑ cortisol).
Target: 5–10% weight loss in overweight PCOS → menstrual cycle restoration.
